libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No additional environment variables or system properties are required. Preserve any existing JVM/module-open settings already present in the lucene-core test build when exercising RamUsageEstimator or MMapDirectory internals.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #5851 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.lucene:lucene-core:6.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Lucene 6.0.0 changed AttributeImpl so custom AttributeImpl test classes must implement reflectWith(AttributeReflector).

Full application-style indexing examples often use optional Lucene modules such as analyzers-common, but lucene-core reachability coverage can use core APIs or test-defined analyzers without adding that dependency.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: lucene-core 6.0.0 is a standalone artifact with no Maven dependencies and no external service requirement; meaningful coverage is reachable with the existing local test scaffold rather than extra dependency, Docker, or environment setup. The reported failure is an API change in AttributeImpl, not missing setup.
